title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Language Lens: Short Write Transcript
summary: A writer emits content before headers, injects unchecked field values, chooses conflicting framing, assumes one Write completes, and cannot prove exact transmitted bytes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the short write transcript increment in canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Drive all header and content bytes through a checked full-write loop and retain an exact bounded transcript for tests.
Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Short Write Transcript · Theory Short Write Transcript
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 3165 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Drive all header and content bytes through a checked full-write loop and retain an exact bounded transcript for tests.
